reason: Task data model provides the core data structure for all todo operations
description: Python dataclass defining the Task entity with fields: id (UUID), description (str), completed (bool), created_at (datetime), and completed_at (optional datetime). Includes validation and serialization methods for JSON storage. No external dependencies, uses only Python stdlib (dataclasses, datetime, uuid).

context_urls.purpose: Official Python dataclasses documentation for defining the Task data structure
Datetime module for task timestamps (created_at, completed_at)
UUID module for generating unique task identifiers

functions.name: Task
functions.returns: Task instance with id, description, completed, created_at, completed_at fields

functions.name: to_dict
functions.returns: Dictionary representation of task for JSON serialization

reason: Storage layer manages JSON file persistence for all task data
description: Module handling todos.json file operations with atomic writes, error handling for missing/corrupted files, and safe JSON encoding/decoding. Provides load_tasks() and save_tasks() functions. Uses pathlib for file operations and contextlib for safe file handling. Handles FileNotFoundError and JSONDecodeError gracefully, initializing empty task list when file doesn't exist.

context_urls.purpose: JSON module for reading and writing task data to todos.json file
Pathlib for modern file path operations and file existence checks
Context managers for safe file operations (with statements)

functions.name: get_storage_path
functions.returns: Path to todos.json (from env var or default)

functions.name: load_tasks
functions.returns: List of Task objects loaded from JSON file
functions.sideEffects: Reads from todos.json file, creates empty list if file missing

functions.name: save_tasks
errors: IOError if file cannot be written
functions.sideEffects: Writes to todos.json file with UTF-8 encoding

reason: CLI parser translates user commands into structured arguments for the application
description: Command-line argument parser using argparse with subcommands: add (description), list, complete (task-id), delete (task-id). Returns parsed namespace with command and arguments. Provides help messages and usage examples. Independent of other modules to allow parallel development.

context_urls.purpose: Argparse API reference for building the CLI argument parser
Argparse tutorial for implementing subcommands (add, list, complete, delete)

io.stdout: Human-readable output, help messages, error messages

reason: Task manager orchestrates CRUD operations and business logic
description: High-level API for task operations: add_task(description), list_tasks(), complete_task(task_id), delete_task(task_id). Handles task lookup by ID, validation, and state transitions. Returns user-friendly error messages (not stack traces). Coordinates between storage and task model layers.

functions.name: complete_task
functions.sideEffects: Updates task completion status and timestamp, persists to storage

reason: Main entry point wires together CLI parser and task manager to execute user commands
description: Application entry point (__main__.py) that parses CLI arguments and dispatches to appropriate task_manager functions. Formats output for terminal display. Catches exceptions and displays user-friendly error messages without stack traces. Exit codes: 0 for success, 1 for user errors, 2 for system errors.

io.stdout: Task lists, confirmation messages, success/error messages
